phases.name: Nudge increment — deprecation signal + migration docs (right-sized, single crafter)

steps.name: Parity/known-gaps statement (US-04) + doc-accuracy guard test
steps.description: Author docs/reference/js-bridge-vs-native-parity.md (Diataxis reference): recommends SwiftInkRuntime, lists known gaps, advises staying on the JS-bridge for them, makes NO 'full parity' claim; references docs/product/ink-feature-reference.md for construct gaps (does NOT duplicate them); lists the API gaps (Combine observation, tag shape [String:String]->[String], error handling currentErrors vs throws); documents the living-parity-backlog convention and names the v3.0.0 endpoint. Author the guard test FIRST (RED): it fails because the doc is absent, then goes GREEN once the doc exists and is consistent with the SSOT.
acceptance_criteria: the statement makes no 'full parity' claim (the substring 'full parity' does not appear as an assertion)
construct gaps reference docs/product/ink-feature-reference.md rather than duplicating the MUST-REJECT list
the API gaps Combine observation, tag shape, and error handling are named
the living-parity-backlog convention is documented and the v3.0.0 endpoint is named
steps.criteria: A new platform-neutral guard test asserts the parity statement (a) exists, (b) references ink-feature-reference.md as the construct-gap SSOT, (c) names the three API gaps, (d) contains no 'full parity' over-claim, (e) names v3.0.0. Full macOS + Linux suite remains GREEN.
steps.scenario_name: US-04 — Honest supported-parity / known-gaps statement (living backlog)
quality_gates: guard test RED before the doc exists, GREEN after
full swift test suite remains GREEN (macOS)
no InkSwift import in the guard test (must run on Linux too)
implementation_notes: No DISTILL AT was authored (DISTILL skipped) — the crafter AUTHORS this guard test fresh in RED; it is doc-accuracy, not oracle-equivalence.

Test reads repo files as TEXT (no InkSwift import) so it runs cross-platform: locate the repo root by walking up from URL(filePath: #filePath) until Package.swift is found, then read docs/reference/js-bridge-vs-native-parity.md and docs/product/ink-feature-reference.md.

Assert the parity doc contains a reference/link to 'ink-feature-reference.md' (construct-gap SSOT) and the strings for the three API gaps (Combine / observation, tag, error), names 'v3.0.0', and does NOT assert 'full parity'.
Swift Testing backtick function-name mandate (CLAUDE.md) — no @Test("...") string labels.
Content source: feature-delta.md US-04 section; construct gaps live in ink-feature-reference.md MUST-REJECT rows (28, 36-39).

steps.name: Migration guide (US-03) + 100%-API-coverage guard test
steps.description: Author docs/how-to/migrate-from-js-bridge.md (Diataxis how-to): the InkStory -> Story/InkCompiler mapping table covering 100% of the InkStory public API, flagging shape differences (tags dict->array, state String->Data, getVariable JXValue->Any?, added throws) and the no-equivalent Combine observation gap; names the v3.0.0 runway; links the parity statement. Author the coverage guard test FIRST (RED): it fails until every InkStory public symbol appears in the guide.
acceptance_criteria: docs/how-to/migrate-from-js-bridge.md exists and maps 100% of the InkStory public API to a native equivalent or an explicit 'no native equivalent'
shape differences are flagged: tags [String:String]->[String], state String->Data, getVariable JXValue->Any?, and added throws on choice/knot/state calls
the Combine reactive-observation gap is stated explicitly as having no native equivalent
the guide names the v3.0.0 runway and links the parity statement
steps.criteria: A new platform-neutral guard test extracts every public func/var/init identifier from Sources/InkSwift/InkStory.swift (parsed as text) and asserts each appears in docs/how-to/migrate-from-js-bridge.md. RED before the guide exists/complete, GREEN after. Full macOS + Linux suite GREEN.
steps.scenario_name: US-03 — Migration guide maps InkStory to the native API
quality_gates: guard test RED before the guide is complete, GREEN after
coverage is 100% of InkStory public members (incl. the observation methods as 'no equivalent')
no InkSwift import in the guard test (runs on Linux)
implementation_notes: Crafter authors this guard test fresh in RED (no DISTILL AT). Parse Sources/InkSwift/InkStory.swift as TEXT for `public (func|var|init)` identifiers within the InkStory type; assert each identifier string appears in the guide markdown.
Locate repo root via URL(filePath: #filePath) walk-up to Package.swift (same helper as 01-01 — consider a small shared test helper if it reduces duplication, but do not over-engineer).

InkStory public surface includes the typo'd `oberservedVariables`, `retainTags`, `registerObservedVariable`, `deregisterObservedVariable` — the guide must mention all (observation ones as the no-equivalent gap).
Backbone mapping table content: feature-delta.md US-03 section.
Depends on 01-01 (the guide links the parity statement).

steps.name: README reposition (US-01) — recommend native, honest gaps
steps.description: Reposition README.md so SwiftInkRuntime is the recommended runtime for new projects: remove the stale 'experimental / plays the first 100 lines of the Intercept' wording; state it executes The Intercept fully and runs on Apple + Linux; add an honest 'Known gaps vs the JS-bridge' note that links the parity statement (01-01) and migration guide (01-02) and makes no 'full parity' claim; reframe the JS-bridge section as the legacy path with a pointer to the migration guide.
acceptance_criteria: README presents SwiftInkRuntime as the recommended runtime for new projects; the 'experimental / first 100 lines' wording is removed
README includes an honest 'Known gaps vs the JS-bridge' note linking the parity statement and migration guide, with no 'full parity' claim
the JS-bridge section remains but is reframed as the legacy path with a pointer to the migration guide
steps.criteria: README.md repositioned per the three ACs. Documentation-only step — no automated test (RED logged SKIPPED / NOT_APPLICABLE). Full suite remains GREEN (unchanged).
steps.scenario_name: US-01 — The README recommends the native runtime (with honest gaps)
quality_gates: the string 'first 100 lines' no longer appears in README.md
links to both new docs resolve (relative paths correct)
implementation_notes: Documentation-only: log RED as SKIPPED with NOT_APPLICABLE (README reposition has no in-process assertion; a doc-lint guard here would be over-engineering per the maintainer's scope).
Current stale text is at README.md top banner (line ~4) + 'Supported features' + 'Getting started' sections that teach InkStory.
Depends on 01-01 + 01-02 so the linked docs exist.

steps.name: Deprecate InkStory (US-02) — @available warning, not error
steps.description: Attach the ADR-015 Option-1 deprecation to the public InkStory type in Sources/InkSwift/InkStory.swift: @available(*, deprecated, message: "InkStory (the JavaScriptCore bridge) is legacy and will be removed in v3.0.0. Migrate to SwiftInkRuntime's Story / InkCompiler — see the migration guide: docs/how-to/migrate-from-js-bridge.md"). Type-level only; message: only (NO renamed: — native Story is a different, non-API-compatible module). It is a WARNING, not an error; no public API removed; the JS-bridge still plays. The ~26 existing InkStory use-sites in our own test targets WILL now emit the deprecation warning — this is accepted (the maintainer chose 'accept them'); do NOT suppress them.
acceptance_criteria: compiling against InkStory emits a Swift deprecation warning whose text names v3.0.0, names SwiftInkRuntime, and references the migration guide
the deprecation is a warning, not an error: existing builds still succeed; no public API is removed; the JS-bridge still plays stories
the removal version in the warning matches the migration guide (01-02) and parity statement (01-01) exactly (v3.0.0)
steps.criteria: The @available attribute is added per ADR-015 Option 1. RED is logged SKIPPED (NOT_APPLICABLE): US-02 is verified by a compiler-warning DEMONSTRATION (the ~26 existing InkStory sites now warn) plus a green build — warnings, not errors — not by an in-process assertion. GREEN: attribute added, full swift test suite still GREEN (no -Werror). Do NOT suppress the own-suite warnings.
steps.scenario_name: US-02 — The JS-bridge API signals it is legacy (deprecation warning)
quality_gates: swift build / swift test still succeed (warnings, not errors)
no public API removed; JS-bridge behaviour unchanged
message text matches v3.0.0 + SwiftInkRuntime + the migration-guide path used in 01-01/01-02
implementation_notes: ADR-015 (Accepted, Option 1): type-level @available on the InkStory class declaration; message: only; axis '*, deprecated' (unconditional, all-platform) => warning not error.
Option struct annotation is OPTIONAL and reach-neutral — DELIVER may skip it; do not add unless trivial.

There is NO -warnings-as-errors anywhere (Package.swift/CI/hook verified) so the build stays green; the ~26 warnings across InkSwiftTests + SwiftInkRuntimeTests oracle uses are ACCEPTED and are the US-02 demonstration.
This step edits the behaviorally-frozen InkSwift module — permitted: the @available annotation is the sole non-behavioral change allowed by the refined invariant (brief.md / ADR-002 refinement).
Ships last so the migration-guide path in the message already resolves (01-02 done).

validation.notes: DISTILL skipped by maintainer decision (doc-accuracy feature; oracle-style ATs N/A). Two doc-accuracy guard tests authored fresh in RED. DEVOPS skipped (guardrail KPI-5 met by construction: no -warnings-as-errors). Single-crafter cohesive delivery per maintainer's ceremony choice.
